params.text:
Welcome to Standard 8

Welcome to Fluids and Nutrition, the knowledge element of Standard 8 of the Care Certificate (2025 framework, England). Helping people to eat and drink well is one of the most basic human needs, and one of the most important things you will support people with on almost every shift. Done well, it protects health, aids recovery and helps people feel valued and comfortable.

  • Hydration: helping people drink enough to stay well.
  • Nutrition: supporting people to eat a balanced diet they enjoy.
  • Dignity: making mealtimes safe, sociable and person-centred.

What this course covers

Over the next pages you will learn why food and fluids matter, how to meet people's hydration and nutrition needs, and how to support them safely and kindly at mealtimes — including those who find swallowing difficult. You will also see how to spot the early signs of dehydration and malnutrition, and what to do when you are worried about someone. This standard is about supporting people to eat and drink well; a separate Food Hygiene course covers safe food handling in full.

Take your time as you work through, and think about the people you support and how each idea applies to them. This e-learning covers the knowledge element of Standard 8 only. To achieve the Care Certificate, your employer must also observe your competence in real work and sign it off — My Care Academy cannot award the Care Certificate on its own.
